statement: Croatia joined the EU on 1 July 2013, the most recent country to accede. Croatia's accession negotiations took over 8 years (2005–2011), covering all 35 chapters. Croatia was required to undertake significant judicial and administrative reforms. No significant opt-outs were granted.
source_name: European Commission — Croatia accession monitoring reports
caveats: Croatia's accession process is instructive but not directly comparable: Croatia had far more chapters requiring reform (judiciary, anti-corruption) than Iceland would, given Iceland's existing EEA implementation.

related_entries.statement: Iceland opened EU accession negotiations in July 2010 under the Social Democrat-led government. Of 33 negotiation chapters, 27 were screened, 11 were formally opened, and none were provisionally closed. The most contentious chapter (13: Fisheries) was never opened. The centre-right government elected in 2013 froze negotiations, and Iceland formally withdrew its application in March 2015.
